description: Savor the smoky perfection of Tandoori Chicken, marinated in a vibrant blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, then expertly grilled over charcoal for an unforgettable flavor. This classic Indian dish boasts tender, juicy chicken with a beautifully charred exterior, making it a show-stopping centerpiece for any dinner. Prepare to impress your guests with this authentic taste of India, right from your own backyard grill.

ingredients.notes: or vegetable oil, heated and cooled

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash and pat dry the chicken pieces. Make deep gashes on the chicken with a sharp knife to allow the marinade to penetrate well. Ensure the chicken is thoroughly dry before proceeding.

instructions.title: First Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the prepared chicken with ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, salt, and half of the Kashmiri red chili powder. Mix well, ensuring the marinade gets into all the gashes. Let it marinate for 30 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Second Marinade (Tandoori Masala)
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together thick plain yogurt, the remaining red chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, turmeric powder, and black pepper. If using, add the red food color. Stir in the cooled mustard oil until well combined.

instructions.title: Combine and Marinate
instructions.text: Add the yogurt-spice mixture from the second marinade to the chicken from the first marinade. Mix thoroughly, ensuring every piece of chicken is completely coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or preferably overnight, for the best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Prepare the Grill
instructions.text: Preheat your charcoal grill to medium-high heat. Arrange the charcoal to create both a direct and an indirect heat zone. Ensure the grill grates are thoroughly cleaned and lightly oiled to prevent the chicken from sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the medium-high direct heat. Grill for 5-7 minutes per side, turning frequently, until lightly charred and smoky. Then, move the chicken to the indirect heat zone, baste generously with melted butter or oil, and continue to grill, turning and basting occasionally, for another 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the tandoori chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, keeping the chicken moist. Garnish with fresh cilantro and lemon wedges. Serve hot with mint chutney, sliced onion rings, and warm naan bread or rice.

tips: For the most tender and flavorful chicken, ensure you marinate it for at least 6 hours, or ideally overnight. The longer the marinade, the better the taste will be.

Using full-fat yogurt is crucial for a rich, creamy marinade that also helps tenderize the chicken. For an even thicker marinade, you can use Greek yogurt or hang regular yogurt in a muslin cloth to drain excess whey.

When grilling, start with direct heat for charring and developing that signature smoky flavor, then move to indirect heat to cook through without burning. Basting with butter or oil throughout the indirect cooking phase keeps the chicken incredibly moist.
article:
## Experience the Smoky Magic: Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken (Indian Dinner Recipes)

Few dishes capture the heart and soul of Indian cuisine quite like Tandoori Chicken. Its vibrant color, succulent meat, and aromatic spices are instantly recognizable and utterly delicious. While traditionally cooked in a scorching hot clay oven called a tandoor, you don't need a specialized oven to bring this culinary masterpiece to your home. With a good charcoal grill, you can achieve that irresistible smoky flavor and tender texture that makes Tandoori Chicken a global favorite. This article will guide you through crafting a sensational Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe, perfect for an authentic Indian dinner. Get ready to impress your family and friends with a dish that’s as fun to make as it is to eat.

## What is Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken?

Tandoori Chicken is a classic Indian dish made by marinating chicken in yogurt and a blend of aromatic spices, then traditionally cooking it at high heat in a tandoor. The term "Tandoori" itself refers to the tandoor oven. When we talk about Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, we're adapting this timeless technique to a backyard charcoal grill. The charcoal imparts a distinctive smoky flavor that beautifully complements the spiced marinade, creating a depth of taste often missing from oven-baked versions. The high, radiant heat of the grill mimics the tandoor's ability to cook the chicken quickly, sealing in juices while crisping the skin, resulting in incredibly moist and flavorful chicken with those characteristic charred edges. It's a fantastic way to enjoy this iconic Indian dinner recipe without needing specialized equipment.

## Ingredients for Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

Crafting the perfect Tandoori Chicken starts with quality ingredients. Here's what you'll need for this delectable Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe:

*   **For the Chicken:**
    *   2-3 lbs chicken pieces (bone-in, skin-on thighs, drumsticks, or a mix are ideal; whole chicken cut into quarters also works).
    *   2 tbsp lemon juice (for the first marinade).
    *   1 tsp salt (for the first marinade).
*   **For the Main Marinade:**
    *   1 cup plain full-fat yogurt (ensure it's thick, drain if necessary).
    *   2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste (freshly made is best).
    *   2 tbsp Kashmiri chili powder (for color and mild heat; adjust to taste).
    *   1 tsp turmeric powder.
    *   1 tsp ground cumin.
    *   1 tsp ground coriander.
    *   1 tsp garam masala.
    *   ½ tsp red food coloring (optional, for classic vibrant red/orange color, or use extra paprika/beetroot powder for natural color).
    *   1 tsp salt (or to taste).
    *   2 tbsp mustard oil or any neutral cooking oil.
*   **For Grilling & Garnish:**
    *   Charcoal briquettes or lump charcoal.
    *   Fresh lemon wedges.
    *   Fresh coriander leaves, chopped.

## How to Make Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken (Step-by-Step)

Making a succulent Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe at home is an exciting culinary adventure. Follow these steps for the best results:

**Step 1: Prepare the Chicken**
Thoroughly wash the chicken pieces and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep slits (2-3 per piece) in the chicken, especially on the thicker parts. This helps the marinade penetrate deeply and ensures even cooking.

**Step 2: First Marinade (Acid & Salt)**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 2 tablespoons of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub this mixture all over the chicken, ensuring it gets into the slits. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes at room temperature. This step helps tenderize the chicken and kickstarts the flavor infusion.

**Step 3: Prepare the Main Marinade**
While the chicken rests, prepare the second, richer marinade. In a separate bowl, whisk together the thick plain yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, garam masala, optional red food coloring, and 1 teaspoon of salt. Stir in the mustard oil (or neutral oil) until well combined.

**Step 4: Marinate the Chicken (Crucial Step!)**
Drain any excess liquid from the chicken after its first marinade. Add the chicken pieces to the main marinade, ensuring each piece is thoroughly coated. Use your hands to rub the marinade into the slits. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or ideally, overnight (up to 24 hours). The longer the marination, the deeper the flavor and more tender the chicken will be.

**Step 5: Prepare Your Charcoal Grill**
About 30-40 minutes before you plan to grill, prepare your charcoal. Arrange the charcoal to create two heat zones: a direct heat zone (coals directly under the grates) and an indirect heat zone (no coals directly under the grates). Aim for a medium-high heat, around 375-400°F (190-200°C). Clean the grill grates and lightly oil them to prevent sticking.

**Step 6: Grill the Tandoori Chicken**
Remove the chicken from the refrigerator about 30 minutes before grilling to bring it closer to room temperature. Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the medium-high direct heat zone. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side until you get some nice char marks. This creates a flavorful crust. Then, move the chicken to the indirect heat zone, close the grill lid, and cook for another 20-30 minutes, flipping occasionally. The cooking time will vary depending on the size of your chicken pieces and grill temperature. The chicken is done when its internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) at its thickest part and the juices run clear. For the last few minutes, you can move them back over direct heat for a final char if desired.

**Step 7: Rest and Serve**
Once cooked, transfer the Tandoori Chicken to a clean platter, cover it loosely with foil, and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum succulence. Garnish with fresh lemon wedges and chopped coriander leaves.

## Pro Tips for the Best Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

Achieving restaurant-quality Tandoori Chicken on your charcoal grill is easy with a few expert tips:

*   **Thick Yogurt is Key:** Use full-fat plain yogurt. If your yogurt is watery, strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter for an hour or two to remove excess moisture. This ensures the marinade sticks to the chicken and doesn't just drip off.
*   **Don't Skimp on Marination:** The longer you marinate, the better the flavor penetration and tenderness. Overnight is truly ideal for this Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe.
*   **Score Deeply:** Those deep slits are not just for show; they allow the marinade to infuse deep into the meat, ensuring every bite is flavorful.
*   **Monitor Grill Temperature:** Tandoori Chicken benefits from initial high heat for searing, followed by indirect heat for even cooking. Avoid burning the outside before the inside is cooked.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and steams the chicken instead of grilling it.
*   **Rest Your Chicken:** This simple step is crucial for juicy chicken. Don't skip it!

## Serving Suggestions for Tandoori Chicken

Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a feast for the senses on its own, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments. Serve it hot with a refreshing mint-coriander chutney and some thinly sliced red onion rings, often sprinkled with chaat masala. Fresh lemon wedges are a must for squeezing over the chicken just before eating, adding a zingy finish. For a complete meal, pair it with warm naan bread or roti to scoop up the flavorful juices, or a side of fluffy basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice. A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with lemon and spices) provides a light, cooling contrast.

## Variations and Substitutions for Tandoori Chicken

While the classic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe is divine, don't hesitate to experiment!

*   **Chicken Cuts:** Boneless chicken thighs work beautifully and cook faster. Chicken breast can also be used, but be careful not to overcook it as it can dry out.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Kashmiri chili powder for heat. For more kick, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or a finely chopped green chili to the marinade.
*   **Flavor Enhancers:** A teaspoon of dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed between your palms, adds an authentic, slightly bitter, and aromatic note to the marinade.
*   **Vegetarian Options:** This marinade works wonderfully with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), large mushrooms, or cauliflower florets. Skewer them and grill until tender and lightly charred.
*   **Alternative Cooking Methods:** While charcoal grilling is superior for flavor, you can also cook Tandoori Chicken in a hot oven (400°F/200°C) or even an air fryer (check specific model instructions) if a grill isn't available. Just remember, you'll miss out on that irreplaceable smoky char.

Embark on a culinary journey right in your backyard with this incredible Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe. It’s a dish that embodies the rich flavors and vibrant spirit of Indian cuisine, promising a memorable dining experience. Enjoy the smoky aroma and the incredibly tender, spiced chicken that will undoubtedly become a staple in your Indian dinner repertoire.
